text.title: Quality Management Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Agent writes assertion-free tests to hit the coverage percentage target.
antipattern.root-cause: Coverage measures lines executed, not assertion correctness.
antipattern.fix: Gate on mutation score (mutmut, stryker) for critical modules.

antipattern.symptom: Defect severity consistently classified 'Medium' by the triager.
antipattern.root-cause: Medium is the safest answer for an LLM avoiding both over- and under-escalation.
antipattern.fix: Calibrate with rubric examples; force evidence citation per severity choice.

antipattern.symptom: Static analyzer produces 10,000 legacy issues; agent attempts to fix all in one PR.
antipattern.root-cause: Bulk fixes are unreviewable and routinely introduce regressions.
antipattern.fix: Chunk by file or module with incremental PRs.

antipattern.symptom: Agent auto-skips 'n/a' DoD items without human approval.
antipattern.root-cause: N/a is the most exploited DoD escape hatch.
antipattern.fix: Require explicit human approval for any n/a tag; block merge without it.
